instructions_for_llm: You are a chip product strategist for Infineon AIROC. Given the current AIROC SKUs, the competitor matrix (strengths/weaknesses vs AIROC), in-flight standards, customer roadmap signals, recent news momentum, and GitHub stack activity, produce a JSON with: feature_gaps (list of {gap, evidence:[urls/notes], priority: 'high|med|low', target_horizon}), sku_recommendations (list of {sku_concept, target_apps, key_features, competitive_response_to:[vendors]}), software_priorities (list of {area, justification, target_horizon}), partnership_targets (list of {partner, rationale}), risks (list of {risk, mitigation}). Be concrete and tie each item to evidence. Save result to data/roadmap_output.json.

standards_in_flight.next_version: Not yet announced (6.3 is the latest adopted release)
in_flight_features: Channel Sounding Inline PCT Transfer (BT 6.3) — analog phase pre-compensation for faster, lower-overhead ranging
Channel Sounding PHY-specific RTT Accuracy (BT 6.3) — per-PHY ranging precision (LE 1M/2M)
Running Out of Bits (BT 6.3) — expanded HCI command/event bitmask for future feature growth
BR/EDR ACP & C/I limit relaxation (BT 6.3) — harmonizes Classic RF limits with LE for simpler dual-mode radios
LE Audio / Auracast adoption continuing to broaden to public venues (ecosystem trend, not a new core feature)
standards_in_flight.infineon_position: AIROC CYW20829 ships Channel Sounding (PBR/RTT baseline from BT 6.0); CYW55513 covers LE Audio. Track BT 6.3 Channel Sounding refinements (Inline PCT Transfer, per-PHY RTT) for next silicon revs.

in_flight_features: Wi-Fi 7 MLO mass deployment in routers/clients
AFC (Automated Frequency Coordination) for 6 GHz standard power
Wi-Fi HaLow (802.11ah) growth in long-range IoT
standards_in_flight.infineon_position: AIROC CYW5557x (Wi-Fi 6E + BT 5.4) competing with Qualcomm/MediaTek/Realtek. No Wi-Fi 7 announced yet — gap vs Synaptics Veros/MediaTek Filogic 380.
standards_in_flight.source_verified: https://www.wi-fi.org/discover-wi-fi/wi-fi-certified-7 (Wi-Fi 7 shipped 2024, 1.1B units shipped in 2026; Wi-Fi 8/802.11bn still pre-certification, target ~2028 per industry commentary)

standards_in_flight.next_version: Matter 1.7 (scope not yet public); Thread next version unverified
in_flight_features: Matter 1.6 — Joint Fabric for cross-ecosystem device sharing (per CSA Unify 2026)
Matter 1.6 — NFC-based commissioning to simplify device setup
Matter 1.6 theme is reliability/interoperability polish, not new device types (per CSA messaging)
Zigbee 4.0 relaunch under CSA — new member testimonials (Legrand, Fortune Brands, Jun-Jul 2026)
Aliro (CSA) digital key — NFC + UWB + BLE, ongoing member adoption (e.g. Nordic testimonial Jun 2026)
standards_in_flight.infineon_position: AIROC has 802.15.4 in CYW55573; Matter SDK supported. Strong vs NXP IW612 / Silicon Labs MG24 in tri-radio stories. Needs to confirm Matter 1.6 (Joint Fabric, NFC commissioning) and Zigbee 4.0 support on roadmap.
standards_in_flight.source_verified: https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/ (Matter 1.6 / Zigbee 4.0 items dated Jun-Jul 2026); Thread version NOT independently confirmed — threadgroup.org has no version number published

standards_in_flight.current_version: FiRa Core 4.0 (announced Dec 2025, with certification program)
standards_in_flight.next_version: Not yet numbered — FiRa Plugfest #2 interoperability validation scheduled Sep 2026
in_flight_features: FiRa Core 4.0 specification + certification program rollout
FiRa Plugfest #2 (Sep 2026, France) — multi-vendor interoperability testing
Aliro digital key (NFC+UWB+BLE) gaining auto OEM commitments
UWB radar / sensing for presence detection
standards_in_flight.infineon_position: AIROC has no native UWB; partners with Qorvo. Watch for combo UWB+BLE parts from Qualcomm, NXP (Trimension), Apple, Samsung. Confirm FiRa Core 4.0 alignment before next combo announcement.
standards_in_flight.source_verified: https://www.firaconsortium.org/ (Core 4.0 announcement Dec 2025; Plugfest #2 Sep 2026 banner)
